questions.question: While performing high-quality CPR on an adult, what action should you ensure is being accomplished?

options.A: Maintaining a compression rate of 90 to 120/min
options.B: Placing hands on the upper third of the sternum
options.C: Allowing the chest to recoil 1 inch
options.D: Compressing to a depth of at least 2 inches

questions.scenario: A 53-year-old woman collapses while gardening. She is unresponsive, is not breathing, and does not have a pulse. A neighbor, who is an emergency medical technician, rushes to her with an AED.
questions.question: When the AED arrives, what is the first step for using it?

options.A: Apply the pads to the patient's chest
options.B: Clear the patient
options.C: Turn on the AED
options.D: Press the shock button

questions.scenario: A 53-year-old woman collapses while gardening. She is unresponsive, is not breathing, and does not have a pulse. A neighbor, who is an emergency medical technician, rushes to her with an AED.
questions.question: After the AED pads are attached to the victim's bare chest, the AED detects ventricular fibrillation. What is the next step when using an AED?

options.A: Check the victim for a carotid pulse
options.B: Follow the AED prompts
options.C: Clear the patient
options.D: Press the shock button

questions.question: What should you do if you need to use an AED on someone who has been submerged in water?

options.A: Do not move the victim, and do not use the AED
options.B: Pull the victim out of the water, but do not use the AED
options.C: Pull the victim out of the water, and wipe the chest
options.D: Do not pull the victim out of the water, but wipe the chest before placing pads

questions.question: How can rescuers ensure that they are providing effective breaths when using a bag-mask device?

options.A: Observing the chest rise with breaths
options.B: Delivering breaths quickly and forcefully
options.C: Always having oxygen attached to the bag
options.D: Allowing air to release around the mask

questions.question: What ratio for compressions to breaths should be used for 1-rescuer infant CPR?

options.A: 5 compressions to 1 breath
options.B: 20 compressions to 2 breaths
options.C: 15 compressions to 2 breaths
options.D: 30 compressions to 2 breaths

questions.question: What is a consideration when you are using an AED?

options.A: You should never remove a transdermal medication patch before applying AED pads
options.B: On a hairy chest, the pads may not stick and may fail to deliver a shock
options.C: AEDs can be used while a victim is submerged in water
options.D: You should not use an AED on someone with an implanted pacemaker

questions.scenario: A 9-year-old child has suddenly collapsed. After confirming that the scene is safe, a single rescuer determines that the child is in cardiac arrest, shouts for nearby help, and activates the emergency response system. He immediately begins performing high-quality CPR. Two additional rescuers arrive to assist in the resuscitation attempt.
questions.question: What actions should occur next, to support a team-based resuscitation attempt?

options.A: 2 rescuers alternate using the AED and giving breaths
options.B: 1 rescuer gives CPR while the other 2 wait for advanced life support to arrive
options.C: 2 rescuers alternate giving high-quality chest compressions
options.D: 2 rescuers operate the AED while the third rescuer gives breaths

questions.scenario: A 9-year-old child has suddenly collapsed. After confirming that the scene is safe, a single rescuer determines that the child is in cardiac arrest, shouts for nearby help, and activates the emergency response system. He immediately begins performing high-quality CPR. Two additional rescuers arrive to assist in the resuscitation attempt.
questions.question: Two rescuers begin high-quality CPR while the third rescuer leaves to get the AED. What action supports 2-rescuer CPR?

options.A: Alternating the AED role every 2 minutes
options.B: Alternating the compressor role every 2 minutes
options.C: Alternating giving rescue breaths every 3 cycles
options.D: Alternating giving shocks every 3 cycles

questions.question: "Members of the team know their boundaries and ask for help before the resuscitation attempt worsens." Match this statement with the most appropriate element of team dynamics listed.

options.A: Knowledge sharing
options.B: Summarizing and reevaluation
options.C: Constructive intervention
options.D: Knowing your limitations

questions.question: A victim with a foreign-body airway obstruction becomes unresponsive. What is your first course of action?

options.A: Start CPR, beginning with chest compressions
options.B: Roll the victim over and perform back blows
options.C: Perform abdominal thrusts
options.D: Perform blind finger sweeps

questions.question: Why is defibrillation important?

options.A: It prevents rearrest from occurring
options.B: It is not important for cardiac arrest
options.C: There is a 100% success rate in regaining a normal cardiac rhythm
options.D: It can restore a regular cardiac rhythm

questions.question: You witness someone suddenly collapse. The person is unresponsive, you hear gasping sounds, and there is no pulse. What should you do next?

options.A: Begin CPR; the gasps are not normal breathing
options.B: Give rescue breaths only, the gasps are not normal breathing
options.C: Monitor the patient; the gasps are considered normal breathing
options.D: Begin CPR, even though gasping is normal breathing

questions.scenario: A middle-aged man collapses. You and a second rescuer go to the victim and find that he is unresponsive, is not breathing, and does not have a pulse.
questions.question: Which action is most likely to positively impact this victim's survival?

options.A: Performing high-quality CPR
options.B: Ensuring scene safety
options.C: Providing rescue breaths
options.D: Checking the pulse frequently

questions.scenario: A middle-aged man collapses. You and a second rescuer go to the victim and find that he is unresponsive, is not breathing, and does not have a pulse.
questions.question: You and another rescuer begin CPR. After a few cycles, you notice the chest compression rate is slowing. What should you say to offer constructive feedback?

options.A: "You need to compress at a rate of 80 to 120 per minute."
options.B: "You need to compress at a rate of at least 120 per minute."
options.C: "You need to compress at a rate of 100 to 120 per minute."
options.D: "You need to compress at a rate of at least 100 per minute."

questions.question: How do you perform chest compressions when providing high-quality CPR to a child victim?

options.A: By compressing the chest at least one third the depth of the chest, about 2 inches (5 cm)
options.B: By compressing the chest at least one fourth the depth of the chest, about 1.5 inches (4 cm)
options.C: By compressing the chest at least two thirds the depth of the chest, about 4 inches (10 cm)
options.D: By compressing the chest at least one half the depth of the chest, about 3 inches (8 cm)

questions.question: When performing CPR on an unresponsive choking victim, what modification should you incorporate?

options.A: There are no modifications to CPR for an unresponsive choking victim
options.B: You do not give breaths to an unresponsive choking victim
options.C: Each time you open the airway, look for the obstructing object
options.D: Attempt a jaw thrust instead of a head tilt-chin lift

questions.scenario: An 8-month-old infant is eating and suddenly begins to cough. The infant is unable to make any noise shortly after. You pick up the infant and shout for help.
questions.question: You have determined that the infant is responsive and choking with a severe airway obstruction. How do you relieve the airway obstruction?

options.A: Encourage the infant to cough
options.B: Give sets of 5 back slaps and 5 chest thrusts
options.C: Begin 2 thumb-encircling hands chest compressions
options.D: Give abdominal thrusts

questions.scenario: An 8-month-old infant is eating and suddenly begins to cough. The infant is unable to make any noise shortly after. You pick up the infant and shout for help.
questions.question: Which action do you perform to relieve choking in an unresponsive infant?

options.A: Perform CPR, and look in the mouth for the obstructing object
options.B: Give sets of 5 back slaps and 5 chest thrusts
options.C: Give sets of 5 abdominal thrusts and 5 back slaps
options.D: Attempt a blind finger sweep when giving breaths to remove the obstructing object

questions.question: Which victim requires high-quality CPR?

options.A: A victim who is unresponsive, has a strong pulse, and is breathing adequately
options.B: A victim who is unresponsive with no normal breathing and no pulse
options.C: A victim who is responsive, has a pulse, and is having trouble breathing
options.D: A victim who is responsive, is having trouble breathing, and has a pulse less than 60/min

questions.question: "The team functions smoothly when all team members know their positions, functions, and tasks during a resuscitation attempt." Match this statement with the most appropriate element of team dynamics listed.

options.A: Clear roles and responsibilities
options.B: Knowing your limitations
options.C: Constructive intervention
options.D: Mutual respect

questions.question: Why is allowing complete chest recoil important when performing high-quality CPR?

options.A: There will be a reduction of rescuer fatigue
options.B: It will reduce the risk of rib fractures
options.C: The heart will adequately refill between compressions
options.D: The rate of chest compressions will increase

questions.scenario: A 67-year-old man is found unresponsive, not breathing, and without a pulse. You and a second rescuer begin performing high-quality CPR.
questions.question: When should rescuers switch positions during CPR?

options.A: Never switch rescuers, and maintain current roles
options.B: Switch rescuers at 5-minute intervals
options.C: Switch rescuers about every 2 minutes
options.D: Switch rescuers when placing the AED pads

questions.scenario: A 67-year-old man is found unresponsive, not breathing, and without a pulse. You and a second rescuer begin performing high-quality CPR.
questions.question: You notice the person giving chest compressions is not allowing for complete chest recoil. What is your next course of action?

options.A: Stand back and await direction from the team leader
options.B: Take over leadership and give direction
options.C: Immediately take over chest compressions
options.D: Tell the compressor you notice decreased chest recoil

questions.question: Rapid defibrillation is a link in the adult Chain of Survival. Why is this important to survival?

options.A: It prevents cardiac arrest
options.B: It prevents respiratory arrest
options.C: It provides normal respiration
options.D: It eliminates the abnormal heart rhythm
